manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: thermometer, bikini, ear, canister, armchair, island, videocassette, snowsuit, blackbird.
Your opponent's team words are: necktie, bulb, blender, walrus, burger, rafter, tail, spear.
Distractor words are: mirror, yacht, bird, footstool, rattlesnake, teapot, daffodil.
The kill words are: escalator.

message.content:
text.CLUE: Retro  
TARGETS: bikini, armchair, videocassette, blackbird
message.reasoning:
We need to give a single-word clue that relates to some of our team's words. Must not be any of the listed words or contain parts of them. Should be semantically related. Provide clue and list of target words. Must be concise.

Our words: thermometer, bikini, ear, canister, armchair, island, videocassette, snowset? actually snowsuit, blackbird.

We can try clue "Cold". Relates to thermometer (measures cold), snowsuit (cold), island? maybe not. Blackbird not cold. Could be "Beach" relates to bikini, island, maybe blackbird? hmm.

Clue "Relaxation": armchair, island, bikini? maybe.

Clue "Vintage": armchair, videocassette, bikini maybe old style, blackbird? hmm.

Clue "Flight": blackbird, island? no.

We need avoid opponent words: necktie, bulb, blender, walrus, burger, rafter, tail, spear. Also distractor words. Kill word escalator cannot be clue.

Clue cannot contain parts of any listed words (like "thermo"? "therm"?). Should be safe.

Pick clue "Summer". Relates to bikini, island, maybe blackbird (songbirds), armchair (outdoor), maybe not thermometer.

But we need specify which target words it relates to. Could be multiple. But the clue must be semantically related. "Summer" relates to bikini, island, blackbird (birds sing), maybe armchair? not too strong. But it's okay.

Make sure clue doesn't contain "sun"? Not a listed word.

Clue "Beach". Relates to bikini, island, maybe blackbird (seagull not blackbird). Could be.

Clue "Cold" maybe safer: thermometer (cold temperatures), snowsuit (cold), island? maybe cold island but not always. Could be blackbird? not. So two words.

Better to give clue that relates to at least 3 to accelerate.

Clue "Travel". Relates to island, armchair (travel chair?), bikini (vacation), videocassette (movies)? Not great.

Clue "Comfort". armchair, snowsuit (comfort in cold), bikini (comfort?), not great.

Clue "Retro". videocassette, armchair (mid-century), bikini (retro style), blackbird (the Beatles song "Blackbird" is retro), maybe.

Let's choose "Retro". Does it contain any part of listed words? No.

Target words: bikini, armchair, videocassette, blackbird. That's four.

Check opponent words for confusion: "bulb" etc. not overlapping.

Distractor: "mirror" could be retro, but okay.

We give:

CLUE: Retro
TARGETS: bikini, armchair, videocassette, blackbird

Make sure not to include extra
